steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three destinations in a series, and each item contains an internal comma (a "where" clause). The question asks which punctuation option handles that correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘cinema and to Moree;’: fails to place a correct separator between the second and third destinations and puts a semicolon before the final where clause. When items in a series already contain commas, using commas to separate them creates ambiguity about where one item ends and the next begins. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘cinema; and to Moree,’: places semicolons between the three items, which is the standard rule for a "complex series" (items with internal punctuation). Produces: "drove to Wellington, where they interviewed locals; to Walgett, where they picketed a segregated cinema; and to Moree, where they staged a protest at a segregated public pool." ✓
  • Choice C — ‘cinema, and to Moree’: uses only a comma before the final item, so the complex series is not separated consistently with semicolons. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘cinema: and to Moree,’: a colon introduces a list or elaboration but is not used to separate items within a series. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
